thig9.1:1.1:
Vaḍḍha’s Mother:
Vaḍḍha, don’t have any
thig9.1:1.2: underbrush-lust ever, in the world, at all.
thig9.1:1.3: Don’t, my little son, have a share in suffering again & again.
thig9.1:3.4:
for the ending of stress.
Vaḍḍha:
thig9.1:4.2: you speak this matter to me.
thig9.1:4.3: I suspect that now in you
thig9.1:4.4:
no underbrush is found.
Vaḍḍha’s Mother:
thig9.1:5.2:
are lowly, middling, or exalted:
No underbrush for them in me
thig9.1:5.3: —not a bit, not the least little bit—
thig9.1:6.1: All effluents are ended for me—
thig9.1:7.2: my mother poked me— because of her sympathy—
thig9.1:7.3: verses connected to the ultimate goal.
thig9.1:8.4: for attaining rest from the yoke.
